paper_title: Study on Birch Pollen Allergy and Gastrointestinal Symptoms

methods.description: This method describes the recruitment and grouping of patients and healthy controls for a study on birch pollen allergy and gastrointestinal symptoms. It includes criteria for inclusion and exclusion, as well as the process for confirming birch pollen allergy.

steps.description: Patients and healthy controls were recruited from the Asthma and Allergy clinic at the Sahlgrenska University Hospital in Gothenburg, Sweden.
steps.searchable_summary: recruit participants from a specific clinic

steps.description: The patients were requested to answer a detailed questionnaire about their birch pollen related symptoms and if and when they had gastrointestinal symptoms.
The diagnosis of birch-pollen allergy was confirmed by a history of rhinoconjunctivitis and a positive skin prick test for birch pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: confirm allergy diagnosis with history and skin prick test

steps.description: Patients were grouped into three categories: those with gastrointestinal symptoms during pollen season, those without, and healthy controls.
steps.searchable_summary: group patients based on symptoms and control status

steps.description: Exclusion criteria were applied to all subjects, including confirmed inflammatory bowel disease, celiac disease, and other specified conditions.
The study was approved by the Ethics Committee of Regional Ethical Review Board in Gothenburg with Dnr. 452-06. Written Informed consent was obtained from the subjects.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain ethical approval and informed consent
